from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Simple phrase mapping for better readability; fall back to a generic pattern.
    # Helper to format dates like YYYY-MM-DD into "DD Month YYYY"
    def _format_date(date_str: str) -> str:
        try:
            parts = date_str.split("-")
            if len(parts) == 3:
                year, month, day = parts
                months = ["January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June",
                          "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December"]
                month_name = months[int(month) - 1]
                # Remove leading zeros
                day_int = int(day)
                return f"{day_int} {month_name} {year}"
        except Exception:
            pass
        return date_str

    phrase_map = {
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o.lower()}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s ground is {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {_format_date(o.strip('\"'))}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o.strip('\"')}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted by {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started his active years in {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made his debut for {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was picked at {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in round {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s player number is {o.strip('\"')}",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"{s} attended {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{o} owns {s}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"The general manager of {s} is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"The chairman of {s} is {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"The stadium of {s} is {o}",
        "universityTeam": lambda s, o: f"The university team of {s} is {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"The team with most championships in {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"The UTC offset of {s} is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"The time zone of {s} is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"The total area of {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group objects by (subject, predicate) to allow aggregation (e.g., multiple clubs)
    agg = {}
    for t in triples:
        key = (t.subject, t.predicate)
        agg.setdefault(key, []).append(t.object)

    def make_agg_phrase(subject: str, predicate: str, objects: list[str]) -> str:
        # join multiple objects with commas and 'and' for the last one
        if len(objects) == 1:
            obj_str = objects[0]
        else:
            obj_str = ", ".join(objects[:-1]) + " and " + objects[-1]

        func = phrase_map.get(predicate)
        if func:
            # For predicates where order matters (e.g., ground), the original mapping already expects (s,o)
            # but when we have multiple objects we need a custom wording.
            if predicate in {"club", "formerTeam", "currentteam", "currentclub"}:
                # Use a natural phrasing that works for multiple objects as well
                return f"{subject} plays for {obj_str}"
            if predicate == "debutTeam":
                return f"{subject}'s debut team was {obj_str}"
            # default mapping works for single or multiple objects (will repeat the pattern)
            return func(subject, obj_str)
        # generic fallback
        return f"{subject} {predicate} {obj_str}"

    # Build phrases for each aggregated key
    phrases = [make_agg_phrase(s, p, olist) for (s, p), olist in agg.items()]

    # Join phrases into a single fluent sentence
    if not phrases:
        return ""
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        sentence = phrases[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]

    # Ensure proper punctuation and capitalization
    sentence = sentence.strip()
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    # Ensure the first character is capitalized (handles cases where sentence may start with a quote)
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

class Triple:

    phrase_map = {
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o.lower()}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s ground is {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",

        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made his debut for {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was picked at {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in round {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"{s} attended {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"The general manager of {s} is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"The chairman of {s} is {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"The stadium of {s} is {o}",
        "universityTeam": lambda s, o: f"The university team of {s} is {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"The team with most championships in {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"The UTC offset of {s} is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"The time zone of {s} is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"The total area of {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group objects by (subject, predicate) to allow aggregation (e.g., multiple clubs)
    agg = {}
    for t in triples:
        key = (t.subject, t.predicate)
        agg.setdefault(key, []).append(t.object)

    def make_agg_phrase(subject: str, predicate: str, objects: list[str]) -> str:
        # join multiple objects with commas and 'and' for the last one
        if len(objects) == 1:
            obj_str = objects[0]
        else:
            obj_str = ", ".join(objects[:-1]) + " and " + objects[-1]

        func = phrase_map.get(predicate)
        if func:
            # For predicates where order matters (e.g., ground), the original mapping already expects (s,o)
            # but when we have multiple objects we need a custom wording.
            if predicate in {"club", "formerTeam", "currentteam", "currentclub"}:
                return f"{subject} plays for {obj_str}"
            if predicate == "debutTeam":
                return f"{subject}'s debut team was {obj_str}"
            # default mapping works for single or multiple objects (will repeat the pattern)
            return func(subject, obj_str)
        # generic fallback
        return f"{subject} {predicate} {obj_str}"

    # Build phrases for each aggregated key
    phrases = [make_agg_phrase(s, p, olist) for (s, p), olist in agg.items()]

    # Join phrases into a single fluent sentence
    if not phrases:
        return ""
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        sentence = phrases[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]

    # Ensure proper punctuation and capitalization
    sentence = sentence.strip()
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    # Helper to format dates like YYYY-MM-DD into "DD Month YYYY"
    def _format_date(date_str: str) -> str:
        try:
            parts = date_str.split("-")
            if len(parts) == 3:
                year, month, day = parts
                months = ["January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June",
                          "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December"]
                month_name = months[int(month) - 1]
                # Remove leading zeros
                day_int = int(day)
                return f"{day_int} {month_name} {year}"
        except Exception:
            pass
        return date_str

    phrase_map = {
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
